methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Stratek Global is a growth-stage nuclear energy company founded by Kelvin Kemm. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 nuclear energy peers: three publicly traded entities (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private firms, including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median approach was not applicable in this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only eight same-sector comparables, and none satisfied the screening criteria for reliable triangulation. Zero companies matched both stage and tier simultaneously; only two occupied adjacent stages, falling short of the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for statistical validity. Reliance on an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of business models and capital intensity within nuclear energy.

In the absence of a sufficiently sized peer-median anchor, this valuation cannot be supported by quantitative triangulation against the available data. A defensible range would require either: expansion of the comparable set through inclusion of adjacent subsectors (e.g., advanced energy or industrial heat), collection of additional private company data at equivalent stage and scale, or application of cash-flow-based methods if forward financials are available. Current data constraints preclude a specific valuation recommendation.
